title: Hide behind Bill Gates books because you're too sad to admit you don't know anything
html:
TL;DR

  • One-line October 2019 self-call-out: Hide behind Bill Gates books because you to sad to admit don't know anything.
  • Yahir performing the awareness-of-the-cope mid-cope — he's reading Gates books, and writing a journal line indicting himself for using the reading as a substitute for facing his own ignorance.
  • Already cross-cited in the Bill Gates entity page as the fall-2019 mockery-pivot — paired with the "watch decoding Bill Gates" line from the previous month, it's the idol-then-roast sequence in 19 days.
  • The line is structural for the mentors-from-afar concept — Yahir's mentor-from-afar pattern operates with full awareness that intense study can be a coping mechanism, not despite it.
  • Sits in the October-November 2019 aphorism cluster with "everyone does as they are told" and "don't do it for money" — Yahir is in self-audit mode all month.

Notes

The note is a teen knowing the trick he's running on himself, and writing it down anyway. "Hide behind Bill Gates books" — the public-figure-author canon as wallpaper for inadequacy. The Decoding Bill Gates Netflix doc dropped in September 2019; Yahir watched it, then reads more Gates, then in mid-October writes the line indicting his own consumption as avoidance. The same brain that runs the mentors-from-afar roster (Masayoshi Son, Chamath, AGM, Jensen) starts here in self-suspicion mode — the input is real, but the motive is also evasion.

The grammar of the line — "too sad to admit don't know anything" — places sadness as the obstacle to admitting ignorance. Not pride, not laziness; sadness. The emotion is the gatekeeper. This is teen-Yahir locating, with surgical precision, the affect that drives intellectual avoidance — and naming it. The same instinct shows up in the Sam arc notes years later: name the emotion that's running the decision.

The hedge in this note is implicit — write the call-out, keep reading the books anyway. It's not a renunciation; it's an acknowledgment. That's the teen era mode — operate while watching yourself operate.

Privacy note

None.

Connections

  • 2019 09 23 write scripture for brenda — the watch decoding Bill Gates paired sibling
  • 2019 10 04 everyone does as told · 2019 10 30 do it for the thing that changes lives · 2019 11 09 it doesnt matter react — fall-2019 aphorism cluster
  • grants pass teen era
  • asking the right question
